description: computeVisible.ts at line 50 lowercases searchQuery once: `const needle = (searchQuery ?? '').toLowerCase();` and then passes that already-lowercased `needle` to searchPredicate, which lowercases both sides again at line 28: `haystack.toLowerCase().includes(needle.toLowerCase())`. The double lowercasing of the needle is functionally idempotent but is wasted work on the hot path (per-note, per-query). More importantly, it means the empty-needle short-circuit in searchPredicate (`if (needle === '') return true;`) is dead in this code path because computeVisible already filters out empty needles before calling. This shows the boundary between the two functions is not crisp: either searchPredicate is the canonical case-fold + substring entry point (and computeVisible should not pre-fold), or computeVisible is responsible for case fold (and searchPredicate should accept already-lowercased inputs and document that). Pick one and document it; otherwise the next refactor will reintroduce a case-sensitivity bug.
